mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60d returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5d returns in >50% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with low policy rates forces markets to price in aggressive future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20d returns remain positive in >65% of instances when inflation exceeds 2.5% and rates are below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms economic resilience and supports corporate earnings expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20d returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% quarterly confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 60d returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising federal funds rates indicates supply-side contraction under monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling INDPRO and rising FEDFUNDS yields positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances.
